head_commit.message:
chore(code-geeko): auth nightly via Max-plan CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN

Swap ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -> CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN in the smoke + run steps so the nightly authenticates on the Max subscription (1-year setup-token) instead of the metered API. No Python changes: the Agent SDK/bundled CLI reads the token from env. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is intentionally not set here -- it outranks the OAuth token in the CLI auth precedence and would shadow it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(code-geeko): run self-test via 'python -m pytest' so codegeeko imports in CI

Bare 'pytest tests/codegeeko' raised ModuleNotFoundError on the codegeeko package in the Actions runner (repo root not on sys.path); it passed locally only because local runs used 'python -m pytest', which prepends CWD. Matches the working 'python -m codegeeko.run' step. The smoke check already confirmed CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN auth works on the Max subscription.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(code-geeko): drain triage query generator fully to avoid aclose race

_run_triage_query returned from the async-for on the first success message, leaving query()'s async generator open; under asyncio.wait_for its teardown raised 'aclose(): asynchronous generator is already running', which the broad except read as a triage failure (state not saved, red run) even though the result was obtained. Now capture the success result and drain to completion, matching the workflow smoke step's proven pattern. Auth + the CI pytest path are already green; collectors surfaced 112 baseline deltas and Task 11.5's fail-safe fired correctly on the crash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci_log:None:29539485282.message: Workflow 'pages build and deployment' failed on 2026-07-16T22:25:22Z (https://github.com/auckiefenstermacher19-cmd/Health-Tracker/actions/runs/29539485282)

head_commit.message:
chore(code-geeko): TEMP-instrument triage None-paths + raw response (Path A diagnosis)

Temporary stderr diagnostics in _run_triage_query to reveal which return-None path fires on the silent triage failure and dump the raw model response (fences? preamble? truncated?). Goes to stderr so it lands in the Actions job log, not the report. To be reverted and replaced with the targeted fix once root cause is confirmed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(code-geeko): TEMP-instrument triage None-paths + raw response (Path A diagnosis)

Temporary stderr diagnostics in _run_triage_query to reveal which return-None path fires on the silent triage failure and dump the raw model response (fences? preamble? truncated?). Goes to stderr so it lands in the Actions job log, not the report. To be reverted and replaced with the targeted fix once root cause is confirmed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci_log:None:29540391059.message: Workflow 'pages build and deployment' failed on 2026-07-16T22:44:13Z (https://github.com/auckiefenstermacher19-cmd/Health-Tracker/actions/runs/29540391059)

head_commit.message:
fix(code-geeko): tolerate markdown-fenced JSON in triage response

Root-caused via run #4 instrumentation: claude-sonnet-5 wraps its decisions payload in a code fence (+ preamble prose) despite 'Respond with ONLY a JSON object', so bare json.loads(raw_result) failed at char 0 and silently returned None -> 'triage failed' + state not saved. _run_triage_query now extracts the outermost {..} object before parsing, tolerating the fence and surrounding prose; no object at all is still a genuine failure. Removed the temporary diagnostic instrumentation. Added a regression test for a fenced+prose-wrapped response (17 triage tests pass locally). Auth, CI pytest path, and the aclose drain fix are already green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

head_commit.message:
chore(code-geeko): TEMP-instrument ci_log runs-API call (silent failure diagnosis)

Temporary stderr diagnostics in run_ci_log_check to reveal the exact HTTP
status/body/permission-headers the Actions GITHUB_TOKEN gets on the runs-API
call, plus token presence. Only stdout is teed to $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY, so these
land in the "Run Code-Geeko" job log, not the report. To be reverted and replaced
with the targeted fix once root cause (401 vs 403 vs decode) is confirmed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

semgrep:.github/workflows/consolidate.ym.message: yaml.github-actions.security.github-actions-mutable-action-tag.github-actions-mutable-action-tag: GitHub Actions step uses a mutable tag or branch reference. Tags and branch names can be silently repointed by the action owner, enabling supply-chain attacks — as seen in the trivy-action and kics-github-action compromises. Pin the reference to a full 40-character commit SHA instead, e.g. `uses: actions/checkout@8ade135a41bc03ea155e62e844d188df1ea18608`.

extra.message: GitHub Actions step uses a mutable tag or branch reference. Tags and branch names can be silently repointed by the action owner, enabling supply-chain attacks — as seen in the trivy-action and kics-github-action compromises. Pin the reference to a full 40-character commit SHA instead, e.g. `uses: actions/checkout@8ade135a41bc03ea155e62e844d188df1ea18608`.

CWE-353: Missing Support for Integrity Check

semgrep:.github/workflows/validate_only..message: yaml.github-actions.security.github-actions-mutable-action-tag.github-actions-mutable-action-tag: GitHub Actions step uses a mutable tag or branch reference. Tags and branch names can be silently repointed by the action owner, enabling supply-chain attacks — as seen in the trivy-action and kics-github-action compromises. Pin the reference to a full 40-character commit SHA instead, e.g. `uses: actions/checkout@8ade135a41bc03ea155e62e844d188df1ea18608`.

extra.message: GitHub Actions step uses a mutable tag or branch reference. Tags and branch names can be silently repointed by the action owner, enabling supply-chain attacks — as seen in the trivy-action and kics-github-action compromises. Pin the reference to a full 40-character commit SHA instead, e.g. `uses: actions/checkout@8ade135a41bc03ea155e62e844d188df1ea18608`.

SHA1 is not collision resistant and is therefore not suitable as a cryptographic signature. Use SHA256 or SHA3 instead.
